instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-type` that evaluates an expression once, checks that it satisfies a given type, and signals a descriptive error if not. The type argument is unevaluated. Returns the value on success.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro assert-type (expr type)
  (let ((g (gensym)))
    `(let ((,g ,expr))
       (unless (typep ,g ',type)
         (error "Type assertion failed: ~S => ~S, not of type ~S"
                ',expr ,g ',type))
       ,g)))
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 (READ)))
            (UNLESS (TYPEP #:G1 'INTEGER)
              (ERROR "Type assertion failed: ~S => ~S, not of type ~S" '(READ)
                     #:G1 'INTEGER))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 (READ)))
            (UNLESS (TYPEP #:G112 'INTEGER)
              (ERROR "Type assertion failed: ~S => ~S, not of type ~S" '(READ)
                     #:G112 'INTEGER))
            #:G112)
